Posted 27 minutes ago

Shopify Designer–Developer for Minimal Luxury Archive Website (Bilingual AR/EN)

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too many applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
  • Verified
    Payment verified
  • Rating is 0 out of 5.
    0
    No feedback yet
  • $600+ spent
  • Location SAU
  • Hourly
  • Intermediate
  • Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Not sure

Title: Shopify Designer–Developer for Minimal Luxury Archive Website (Bilingual AR/EN) --- Project Overview: We are building Ateeq — a curated, archive-led luxury resale platform focused on archived designer pieces. This is not a typical e-commerce store. Each product functions as: - A product listing (pre-sale) - A permanent archive record (post-sale) The website should feel closer to an editorial archive or digital museum than a marketplace. --- Current Status: - Brand identity complete (logo, color palette, typography) - Content written for key pages (About, Authentication, etc.) - Clear structure and product logic defined - Homepage and product page direction will be provided (structured outlines) - Temporary Shopify theme used as placeholder for coming soon page --- What We Are Looking For: A Shopify designer–developer (hybrid) who can: - Recommend the right Shopify theme - Design clean, minimal layouts within that system - Fully implement the website in Shopify This role requires both: - Strong Shopify development skills - Good design judgment (minimal, editorial, luxury feel) --- Scope of Work: ### 1. Theme Selection & Setup - Recommend and implement the most suitable Shopify theme - Ensure flexibility and scalability --- ### 2. Homepage Design & Build - Design and implement homepage based on provided structure - Maintain strong hierarchy and minimal layout --- ### 3. Archive Product (Collection) Pages - Design product listing pages that feel curated, not marketplace-driven - Keep filtering clean and minimal --- ### 4. Product Page (Archive Passport) — Core Requirement Each product page must function as a permanent archive record. Includes: - Structured product information (editorial + technical) - Pre-sale vs post-sale behavior - Clean, minimal layout Each product page will also be linked to a QR code (printed on physical tags), so the page must function as a long-term accessible record. --- ### 5. Private Ownership Layer After purchase, additional sections should be visible only to the owner (logged-in customer): - Bill of sale - Serial number - Packing / fulfillment media -Price Requires: - Customer-based access logic - Conditional rendering --- ### 6. Customer Accounts & Access - Implement Shopify customer accounts (login/logout) - Enable conditional visibility based on logged-in user - Support owner-specific access to private product information --- ### 7. Metafields & Product Data Structure - Implement structured Shopify metafields for: - Public archive data - Private ownership data - Internal product data - Ensure clean, scalable structure --- ### 8. Static Pages - Build clean layouts for: - About Ateeq - Authentication - Archive Passport - Shipping - Returns - …etc --- ### 9. Bilingual Implementation (Arabic + English) - Implement full bilingual structure (Arabic + English) from day one - Ensure proper layout handling, including RTL (right-to-left) for Arabic - Maintain consistent structure and navigation across both languages - Ensure typography, spacing, and layout work well in both languages - Ensure product pages work seamlessly in both languages, especially when accessed via QR code on mobile devices --- ### 10. Currency - Store will be based in SAR (Saudi Riyal) - Structure should support clean price display and allow future multi-currency support if needed --- ### 11. Checkout & Purchase Flow - Ensure clean and functional Shopify checkout experience - Validate full purchase flow from product page to order completion - Support standard Shopify payment integrations, including Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) options (e.g. Tabby, Tamara) --- ### 12. Scalability - Structure must support: - Long-term archive growth - Selling across multiple channels (website, physical POS, and third-party platforms) - Maintaining a single product record per item --- Site Structure & Page Direction: The website will follow a minimal, structured navigation: - Archive (main product area) - Journal (future-ready) - About - Services - Information We will provide structured outlines for: - Homepage - Product page (Archive Passport) - Key static pages The developer is expected to translate this into clean, well-designed layouts. ### Site Structure & Page Direction The website will follow a minimal, structured navigation and clear content hierarchy. **Static Pages:** - Home page - About - Authentication - Archive Passport - Packaging & Shipping - Returns - Contact (including WhatsApp link) -Join the Archive (Subscribe to the mailing list ) Content for these pages will be provided. --- **Archive (Dynamic Product Collection):** The main product area (“Archive”) will include: - All Pieces - Available Pieces - Archived Pieces (sold items) Products should be filterable by structured attributes such as: - Brand / House - Era - Category - Other relevant attributes as needed The Archive should feel curated and editorial, not like a typical marketplace. --- **Navigation Structure:** Top-level navigation should remain clean and minimal, with Archive as the primary entry point for browsing products. The developer is expected to translate this structure into a clear, intuitive navigation system across both desktop and mobile. --- **Page Direction:** Structured outlines for: - Homepage - Product page (Archive Passport) - Static pages will be provided. The developer is responsible for translating these into clean, well-designed layouts aligned with the overall minimal, editorial direction. --- Design Direction: - Minimal - Editorial - Luxury - Calm, structured layout - No marketplace patterns (no “best sellers”, “drops”, etc.) --- ### Responsive & Mobile Experience - The website must be fully responsive across devices - Special attention should be given to mobile experience, as users may access product pages via QR codes - Mobile layouts should maintain the same minimal, clean, editorial feel as desktop --- Important Notes: - This is not a standard Shopify store - Focus is on structure, clarity, and long-term scalability - Business configuration (payments, VAT, shipping) will be handled internally --- Requirements: - Strong Shopify experience (Liquid, themes, metafields) - Ability to design clean, minimal layouts (not just implement) - Experience with premium themes - Understanding of conditional logic for user-specific content --- Nice to Have: - Experience with luxury or fashion brands - Editorial-style websites - Experience with bilingual (Arabic/English) Shopify builds --- Project Approach: We prefer a phase-based delivery, for example: - Phase 1: Theme selection + homepage - Phase 2: Product page + core logic - Phase 3: Remaining pages + polish Open to recommendations on structuring phases. --- Timeline: Flexible, but ideally within 4–6 weeks. --- Budget: Open to proposals based on scope and experience. --- To Apply: Please include: 1. 2–3 relevant Shopify projects (minimal / editorial preferred) 2. Which theme you would recommend and why 3. How you would approach: - A product page that works as both a store and an archive - Showing some content only to the buyer --- Important: We are not building a typical Shopify store. We are building a long-term archive platform with commerce capabilities.

  • Proposals: 20 to 50

We are a fast-growing e-commerce company in fashion dropshipping on Shopify and are looking for an accurate and proactive Backend Virtual Assistant (VA) to support us with daily operational processes. Your responsibilities: Performing quality checks on products, data, and internal processes Managing and keeping sheets and documentation up to date Calculating and analyzing BER (Break-even ROAS) Checking and monitoring Parcel Panel / shipment tracking Identifying discrepancies and proactively thinking about improvements Supporting various backend and operational tasks What we are looking for: Strong analytical and organizational skills Experience with Google Sheets and/or Excel Accurate, structured, and able to work independently Experience in e-commerce or logistics is a plus Good communication skills (Dutch and/or English) What we offer: Flexible, fully remote working opportunities Working within an ambitious and growing e-commerce team Salary: $6–$7 per hour Growth opportunities within the company Interested? Send us a short motivation and relevant experience. We look forward to your response! If you’re not qualified for this role and you respond anyway, you will be blocked.

This project requires someone who understands modern eCommerce trends, winning product research, and conversion-focused design. The goal is to build a clean, trustworthy, and sales-driven store. 🎯 Responsibilities: Design and customize a professional Shopify store (premium look & feel) Optimize store layout for high conversion rates Import and list winning/quality products (with proper descriptions) Write SEO-optimized product titles and descriptions Set up collections, navigation, and homepage sections Install and configure essential Shopify apps Optimize images and product pages for better engagement Ensure mobile responsiveness and fast loading speed ✅ Requirements: Proven experience in Shopify dropshipping Strong knowledge of Shopify themes and customization Experience with product research & listing Understanding of Shopify SEO Ability to create high-converting product descriptions Good communication and ability to meet deadlines 💡 Bonus Skills (Optional but Preferred): Experience with Facebook Ads / TikTok Ads Knowledge of upselling apps (e.g., bundles, cross-sells) Branding and logo design skills 📦 Project Scope: Full store design/setup Upload and optimize 10–30 products (or more if needed) Delivery within agreed timeline 💰 Budget: Flexible (based on experience and quality of work) 📩 To Apply: Please include: Examples of Shopify stores you’ve designed Your approach to building a high-converting store Estimated delivery time

We are a growing e-commerce brand looking for a Shopify Product Page Maker to help us create effective product pages optimized for conversion. We regularly launch new products and need someone capable of creating clear, professional, and well-structured product pages on Shopify. The product pages must be written and formatted in French, so the ability to translate and adapt content into natural French is important. Your main mission: create product pages optimized for sales. • Create product pages on Shopify • Structure the page for conversion (images, descriptions, selling points, FAQ, etc.) • Translate and adapt product content into clear and natural French • Integrate visuals and content provided by our team • Ensure the page is clean, easy to read, and optimized for mobile • Follow a predefined product page structure The workload may vary depending on the period. Some weeks may require several product pages, while other weeks may be quieter. We are therefore looking for someone responsive and reliable when new products need to be launched quickly.

Requirements: Proven experience building one-product Shopify stores Strong focus on conversion (not just design) Clean, fast-loading, mobile-first layout Must have Apple Pay and fast checkout setup Ability to implement upsells, urgency, and trust elements What we need: One-page product store (landing page style) Optimised for mobile (very important) Fast checkout integration (Apple Pay etc.) Basic branding and layout (we will provide product images/videos and logo) Please include in your proposal: Examples of one-product stores you’ve built (Shopify or other platforms) Links to live stores if possible Your turnaround time Your cost for full completion We are looking to move quickly and will choose someone who can deliver fast and understands conversion-focused design. **Shopify Developer Needed – Single-Product Wellness/Supplement Store** --- **About the Project** We are launching a new direct-to-consumer brand selling a wellness supplement product (powder format). We need a polished, conversion-focused Shopify store built from scratch, inspired by the look and feel of https://www.aplusnutri.com/ — a clean, single-product store with a strong brand identity, trust-building elements, and a smooth checkout experience. This is a real, funded project with a hard deadline. We are NOT looking for someone who needs weeks to deliver — the store must be fully completed and ready to launch by Thursday 17 April 2026. **Please only apply if you can commit to this deadline.** --- **What We Need** - Custom Shopify store setup (theme selection or custom theme build) - Single hero product page with high-converting layout: - Product imagery/mockup integration - Ingredient highlights / benefit sections - Reviews / social proof section - FAQ section - Subscription/bundle upsell options (if applicable) - Announcement bar with rotating promotional messages - Email opt-in section - Mobile-first, fully responsive design - Multi-language support (at minimum English + one other language) - Multi-currency support - ParcelPanel or similar order tracking integration - Basic policy pages (shipping, returns, privacy, terms) - Payment gateway setup (Stripe, PayPal, Shop Pay, etc.) - Page speed optimisation --- **Reference Site** Please review this site before applying: https://www.aplusnutri.com/ We want a similar feel: minimal, premium, wellness-oriented. We are NOT looking for a copy — we want our own branding and identity built in a similar style. --- **Ideal Candidate** - Proven experience building Shopify stores for DTC (direct-to-consumer) supplement or wellness brands - Strong eye for clean, premium design — not just a developer, but someone who understands branding - Experience with Shopify's Online Store 2.0 / Liquid - Familiarity with conversion rate optimisation (CRO) best practices - Ability to work independently and communicate clearly in English - Available to start immediately and deliver by Thursday 17 April 2026 - Portfolio of similar projects is required — please share links to Shopify stores you have built --- **Nice to Have** - Experience with Klaviyo or other email marketing integrations - Knowledge of Shopify subscription apps (e.g. Recharge, Seal Subscriptions) - Experience with upsell/cross-sell apps (e.g. ReConvert) --- **Project Details** - Budget: [Your budget here] - Deadline: Thursday 17 April 2026 — no exceptions - Platform: Shopify - Communication: Daily updates via message; available for quick calls if needed --- **To Apply** Please include: 1. Confirmation that you can deliver by Thursday 17 April 2026 2. Links to 2–3 Shopify stores you have built (ideally supplement or wellness brands) 3. A brief note on your approach — how would you tackle this project? 4. Your cost breakdown Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.

  • Proposals: 5 to 10

  • Est. budget: $100.00

I’m looking for a developer who can act more like a long-term tech partner rather than just completing isolated tasks. I manage multiple online stores and web projects, and I need someone who can consistently support with improvements, fixes, and new feature implementation across different platforms. A large portion of the work is Shopify-based, including store enhancements, custom functionality, and resolving issues that come up during day-to-day operations. Alongside that, I also have WordPress websites that need ongoing updates, restructuring, and occasional custom builds. For projects that require more flexibility, I use custom solutions built with React and Node, so experience with modern JavaScript frameworks is important. I’m not looking for someone who just completes tickets. I prefer working with someone who can understand the business side of things, suggest improvements, and help make better technical decisions when needed. We’ll start with a small task, and if things go smoothly, this can turn into a consistent working relationship. If you apply, keep it simple. Share a few relevant projects and what you specifically handled in them. Bonus if you: Have worked on scaling or optimizing e-commerce stores Are comfortable with API integrations and third-party tools Can suggest improvements instead of waiting for instructions Understand performance, speed, and clean architecture Have experience handling both quick fixes and larger features

  • Est. budget: $150.00

Hi, I’m looking for a Shopify developer who can help improve my product pages – mainly structure, usability and mobile behavior. Important upfront I’m using a custom-built Shopify theme, so I need someone who is comfortable working directly in code (Liquid, HTML, CSS, JS) – not just page builders. What I currently have Product descriptions are too long and poorly formatted Text blocks have too much spacing Content appears duplicated on mobile The "full description" and other sections sometimes show twice If I hide/show elements, it breaks layout consistency across desktop/mobile What I want instead 1. Modular content structure Ability to build product pages like: text → image → text → image (flexible order) Not one long text block Easy to maintain per product 2. Custom product tabs (per product) Tabs like: Description Ingredients / Details Preparation / Usage Important: content must be individually editable per product, not global 3. Collapsible / accordion sections Option to shorten long text “Read more” / expandable sections Clean UX, especially on mobile 4. Fix mobile issues Remove duplicate content on mobile Ensure consistent display between desktop & mobile No breaking when toggling sections on/off 5. Improve visual layout Reduce spacing between elements Make layout more compact and structured More like a mix of: text + image blocks small info sections instead of long paragraphs Reference / Direction I like the structure of Pure Tea product pages: Shorter content blocks Better mix of text and visuals Cleaner and more modern layout Tech stack Shopify (custom theme) Liquid / HTML / CSS / JavaScript No heavy page builder preferred What I need from you Suggest best approach (custom sections vs. metafields vs. app) Implement clean, reusable solution Keep it simple to maintain (I update products myself) Please see loom to what I need here: https://www.loom.com/share/fcc1fbb428284753b48339e46b719f84

Hi there, I’m looking for an experienced Meta Ads Specialist with a strong focus on Shopify tracking & pixel setups. I’ve been running Shopify stores and Meta Ads for over 2 years, so I’m very familiar with the standard setup process (pixel installation, events manager, domain verification, etc.). However, I’m currently running into a persistent issue connecting my pixel to new ad accounts, and I can’t seem to resolve it. That’s where you come in. What I need help with: Troubleshooting Meta Pixel connection issues Ensuring proper connection between: Shopify store Meta Business Manager Ad accounts Diagnosing why the pixel is not linking to new ad accounts Fixing potential issues with: Permissions / access Business Manager structure Data sources Event tracking Making sure everything is set up clean and scalable for multiple ad accounts Important context: I’ve done this setup many times before, so this is not a beginner issue The basic steps are all correctly followed (as far as I can tell) I’m specifically looking for someone who can debug deeper issues and spot what I’m missing Requirements: Proven experience with Meta Ads & Shopify integrations Strong understanding of: Meta Business Manager structure Pixels, datasets, and events Domain verification & Aggregated Event Measurement Experience with multi-ad-account setups Ability to quickly diagnose and fix technical issues Nice to have: Experience working with scaling e-commerce brands Familiarity with tools like Funnelish or advanced tracking setups Goal: Fix the issue fast and ensure a stable, scalable tracking setup going forward.

  • Est. budget: $150.00

Looking for a highly experienced dropshipping expert to help me build a profitable car detailing dropshipping business focused on the Brazilian market. I already have the website created with Wordpress and with Woocommerce. The ideal candidate must know how to identify low-cost, high-potential products from China or other reliable low-cost sourcing locations, and also know how to set up the full dropshipping workflow on a WordPress website with WooCommerce. NO SHOPIFY!!!!! Main goals of this project • Research and identify the 25 best dropshipping products related to car detailing • Focus only on products with strong potential for the Brazilian market • Prioritize very low-cost products with good profit margins • Work only with reliable, highly rated suppliers • Add at least 5 selected products to my WooCommerce store • Teach me how to manage the process by myself after setup The freelancer should be able to: • Research winning car detailing products • Source products from China or other low-cost locations • Evaluate supplier quality, ratings, delivery reliability, and product costs • Recommend products suitable for dropshipping into Brazil • Set up the dropshipping workflow on WordPress + WooCommerce • Add at least 5 products to my website, including product details • Explain the full process clearly so I can repeat it myself later The products must be: • Related to car detailing • Suitable for the Brazilian car detailing market • Low cost but with good profit potential • Supplied by reliable and highly rated suppliers • Available from China or another low-cost source • Practical to ship and not overly fragile, bulky, or risky for returns About the freelancer candidate • A real expert in dropshipping • Experienced with WooCommerce and WordPress • Familiar with product research and supplier selection • Professional, reliable, and easy to communicate with • Able to set up the full dropshipping process • Able to teach and explain each step in a simple way At the end of the project, I expect: • A list of 25 recommended car detailing products • Supplier links and justification for each recommendation • At least 5 products added to my WooCommerce store • Guidance on how to manage orders, suppliers, and fulfillment - Tutorial in Word • A simple step-by-step explanation so I can continue by myself - Tutorial in Word To apply, please include • Examples of Your experience with dropshipping and WooCommerce projects you have completed • Your process for selecting winning products and reliable suppliers • Your knowledge of the Brazilian market, if any • Estimated timeline and what exactly is included in your service Please do not apply unless you can provide: • Proven experience in dropshipping and WooCommerce experience • Real specific supplier research, not generic product lists • Clear communication in English • A practical teaching approach

I am launching a premium skincare brand called Zevoria™, and I am looking for a skilled Shopify expert to build a high-converting, clean, and luxury-style online store. This is NOT a basic dropshipping store. I want a professional brand look that feels like a real skincare company. Project Scope: - Build a Shopify store from scratch (or redesign existing store) - Create a clean, modern, and premium homepage - Design a high-converting product page (focused on results and simplicity) - Ensure full mobile optimization - Implement a simple and smooth user experience Brand Direction: - Premium, clean, minimal design - Colors: green, gold, white (branding already created) - Focus on “glow”, natural skincare, and confidence Important: - The store must be conversion-focused, not just visually nice - I want someone who understands e-commerce and customer behavior - Experience with skincare or beauty brands is a plus What I need from you: - Examples of Shopify stores you have built (preferably skincare/beauty) - Your approach to building a high-converting store - Estimated timeline Goal: Build a store that looks like a premium brand and is optimized to convert visitors into customers. If you read this, start your proposal with the word “ZEVORIA,” so I know you paid attention.

structured data) •Configure collections, navigation, filters, and on-site search •Install, configure, and troubleshoot Shopify apps •Monitor site performance, page speed, and mobile experience •Run About Us: UX-driven team building D2C Shopify stores (beauty tech). Focus: conversion, experimentation, performance. Intro: We’re looking for a long-term Shopify development partner who goes beyond execution. Someone who actively thinks along, improves our tech stack, and contributes to building high performing user experiences—not just shipping tickets. Role: - Implement A/B test variants (UI, UX, content) - Collaborate closely with our UX Designer - Optimize themes (performance, structure) - Ensure stability of key flows (Cart, Checkout, PDP) What We Strictly Require: 1. Proactive Communication If something is unclear—or you see a better way—pause and communicate. 2. Clean Code & Architecture Your code should be readable, maintainable, and scalable. 3. No “Blind” AI Coding AI can assist—but not replace understanding. You must fully own and understand the code you deliver. 4. Impact Awareness Before pushing changes, you must ensure that critical flows (Cart, Checkout, PDPs) remain fully functional. Technical Skills: - Expert in Shopify development - Strong experience with Liquid, HTML5, CSS3, and Vanilla JavaScript - Shopify Legacy Themes vs. Online Store 2.0 - Ability to clearly document changes for team transparency Engagement Details: - Long-term collaboration - Ongoing A/B testing and optimization work - Flexible workload based on experiment pipeline

  • Rating is 0 out of 5.
    0
    No feedback yet
  • $0 spent
  • Location United States
  • Hourly: $8.00 - $11.00
  • Intermediate
  • Est. time: More than 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week

We're looking for a **Developer / Funnel Builder** to own the build and delivery of high-converting landing pages, listicles, advertorials, and sales funnels on Shopify. This is a hands-on execution role. You'll work closely with our Creative Strategists, Copywriters, and Graphic Designers to bring funnel concepts to life — fast, clean, and optimized for performance. **Location:** Remote (Asia or LATAM preferred) **Type:** Full-Time Contractor **Reports to:** Head of Direct Response & CMO --- **What You'll Do** **Funnel Building & Development** - Build landing pages, listicles, advertorials, and sales funnels on Shopify using Liquid, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript - Work with tools like GemPages, Replo, or custom Shopify sections as needed - Translate wireframes, copy docs, and design assets into fully functional, high-converting pages - Ensure pages are mobile-optimized, fast-loading, and pixel-perfect - Implement tracking (pixels, UTMs, conversion events) correctly on every page **Collaboration & Execution** - Work closely with Creative Strategists (who define the funnel concept and angle) - Work closely with Copywriters (who provide the copy and messaging) - Work closely with Graphic Designers (who provide visuals and assets) - Turn around funnel builds quickly — we test new funnels weekly - Communicate progress, blockers, and timelines proactively **AI Integration** - Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, etc.) to accelerate your workflow — code generation, debugging, optimization suggestions - Know when AI output is good and when it's wrong — you're the quality filter, not the AI - Continuously improve your process by integrating AI where it adds speed without sacrificing quality **Optimization & Iteration** - Implement A/B test variations quickly when needed - Make updates and iterations based on performance feedback from the media buying team - Maintain a library of reusable templates and components for faster builds - Document your builds so others can understand and maintain them --- ### **What We're Looking For** **Must-Haves:** - 2+ years experience building landing pages, funnels, or custom Shopify pages - Strong proficiency in Shopify Liquid, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript - Experience with page builders (GemPages, Replo, PageFly, or similar) - Experience with DTC / ecommerce brands — you've built pages designed to convert, not just look pretty - Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with weekly deadlines - Strong attention to detail — pixel-perfect, mobile-first builds - Proactive communicator — you flag blockers early and keep the team updated - Experience using AI tools in your workflow — and knowing when to trust them and when not to **Nice-to-Haves:** - Experience with conversion rate optimization (CRO) — you understand what makes a page convert - Experience building advertorials or long-form sales pages - Familiarity with tracking setup (Meta Pixel, Google Tag Manager, Triple Whale) - Experience working with creative or marketing teams (not just solo dev work) - Portfolio of DTC landing pages or funnels you've built --- ### **What Success Looks Like** **First 30 Days:** - Fully onboarded — understand our brand, product, and funnel strategy - Delivered 2-3 landing pages or funnel builds based on team briefs - Established workflow with Creative Strategists, Copywriters, and Designers - Set up reusable templates or components for faster future builds **First 90 Days:** - Delivering 4-5 funnel builds per month consistently - Turnaround time from brief to live page is under 5 business days - Pages are clean, fast, mobile-optimized, and tracking is always correct - You're proactively suggesting improvements — not just executing --- ### **Working Structure** - Full-time dedicated role — 40 hours/week - Remote - Must have overlap with US or EU working hours for collaboration - Slack response time: within 1 hour during working hours - Daily priorities posted in #daily-priorities (BOD and EOD) - Weekly sync with Head of Direct Response --- ### **Tools You'll Use** - Shopify (Liquid, themes, custom sections) - GemPages, Replo, or similar page builders - Figma (receiving designs) - Google Docs / Notion (receiving briefs and copy) - Slack (communication) - AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, etc.) - Google Tag Manager / Meta Pixel / Triple Whale (tracking)

Shopify Data Architect – Technical AV Catalog & Layered Navigation Setup

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too many applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
  • Verified
    Payment verified
  • Rating is 5.0 out of 5.
    5.0
    4.97 Stars, based on 45 feedbacks
  • $40K+ spent
  • Location Australia
  • Fixed price
  • Expert
  • Est. budget: $1,000.00

Project Overview CHT Solutions is a premium Home Theatre and Hi-Fi retailer in Australia. We are transitioning from a "copy-paste" product catalog to a high-performance, structured data model. We need an expert to build a robust Metafield and Metaobject-based architecture that enables advanced layered navigation (filters) similar to Crutchfield or major electronics retailers. The Challenge Our current listings lack structured data. Customers cannot effectively filter for technical specs (e.g., "9.4 channel receivers," "Dirac Live support," or "8K/120Hz compatibility"). We need to move away from using Tags for filtering and implement a scalable Custom Data system. Scope of Work Data Schema Design: Audit our core categories (AV Receivers, Projectors, Speakers, Subwoofers) and define a standardised Metafield schema for each. Metaobject Implementation: Set up Metaobjects for "Brands" to store reusable data (Warranty info, Brand Logos, Support links) across all products. Advanced Filtering: Implement and configure a professional filtering app (e.g., Smart Product Filter & Search or Boost AI) or optimise the Shopify Search & Discovery app using the new Metafield data. Bulk Migration Strategy: Create a CSV/Notion mapping template so our internal VA can backfill data into the new structure without breaking the site. Performance Optimisation: Ensure the new filtering logic does not impact page load speeds or create SEO "filter-bloat." Requirements Shopify Expert: Deep knowledge of Metafields, Metaobjects, and Liquid. Data Strategy: Proven experience building complex product filters for technical catalogs (Electronics, Automotive, or Industrial). SEO Mindset: Understanding of how to handle canonical tags and indexation for filter pages. AV Knowledge (Bonus): If you know the difference between Atmos and Auro-3D, or Gain on a projector screen, you are our ideal candidate. Application Question (Must Answer) "If a product belongs to two categories with different technical specs for example, a Powered Speaker that is both a 'Bookshelf Speaker' and an 'Active Speaker', how would you structure the Metafields to ensure relevant filters appear for both?" $1000 is a placeholder budget, come up with your own to suit the brief
